title: Foxbook transparency-log leaf taxonomy (v1)
description:
The set of event shapes written to tl_leaves.leaf_data. Schema revisions (additive within v1.x per ADR 0004; $id never changes):
  - v1.0 — `agent-key-registration` only.
  - v1.1 — adds `revocation` per Day-6 PR B (ADR 0004 addendum-1: delete-on-revoke; recovery-key-signed leaf body via canonical.ts per ADR 0005).
  - v1.2 — adds `signing-key-registration` (signing-key rotation; recovery-key-signed; same DID, new ed25519 public key). Pins the shape ahead of the rotation flow's implementation per public commitment on A2A Discussion #1803 (desiorac thread, 2026-04-30T10:53Z). The flow itself lands in a follow-up PR.
Explicit deferrals (must not land silently before their target):
  - `claim-event` (shape TBD) → Day 5+, pairs with the claim flow state machine.
  - `manifest-version-diff` → week 2+, after manifests_versions has callers.
  - `tier-upgrade` → v1.1+ (filed for v1.1 to close the Tier-2 attestability asymmetry per Day-6 PR C body).
  - Firehose `hire.settled`/`hire.failed`/`delegation.announced`/`claim.verified`/`revocation.recorded` events are NOT leaves — they live in firehose_events and reference leaves by inclusion proof.
All leaf shapes $ref shared primitives in x-foxbook.v1.json (did, ed25519PublicKeyHex, recoveryKeyFingerprint) rather than redefining them — prevents the "two definitions of did" drift bug.

revoked_key_hex.description: The ed25519_public_key_hex from a prior agent-key-registration leaf at the same `did`. Pointer-like binding (not payload duplication) per ADR 0004 addendum-1.

recovery_key_signature.description: Compact-JWS EdDSA signature over canonical.ts({leaf_type, did, revoked_key_hex, revocation_timestamp, reason_code?}) — every leaf field except this signature itself. Signed by the recovery-key whose SHA-256 matches the prior registration's recovery_key_fingerprint.

reason_code.description: Optional; absent in the canonical signing input when omitted (canonical.ts skips undefined keys). Additive per ADR 0003 — adding new reason codes is in-place v1.x; removing values is breaking and requires a v2 schema.

signingKeyRegistration.description: Signing-key rotation leaf. Registers a NEW agent signing key for an EXISTING DID, replacing prior_ed25519_public_key_hex with new_ed25519_public_key_hex. Recovery-key-signed by the recovery-key whose SHA-256 fingerprint matches the prior agent-key-registration leaf's recovery_key_fingerprint (the recovery key itself is the durable identity anchor; signing keys rotate, the recovery key does not). The verifier resolves the prior leaf via (did, prior_ed25519_public_key_hex) and validates the JWS against that leaf's recovery_key_fingerprint.

prior_ed25519_public_key_hex.description: The ed25519_public_key_hex from a prior agent-key-registration (or signing-key-registration) leaf at the same `did`. Pointer-like binding to the leaf being rotated FROM.

new_ed25519_public_key_hex.description: The new agent signing key being registered. After this leaf is appended + included in an STH, AgentCards signed with this key validate as `verified` for the same DID; cards still signed with prior_ed25519_public_key_hex validate as `unverified` with reason_code `superseded`.

recovery_key_signature.description: Compact-JWS EdDSA signature over canonical.ts({leaf_type, did, prior_ed25519_public_key_hex, new_ed25519_public_key_hex, published_at}) — every leaf field except this signature itself. Mirrors the revocation leaf's signing input convention so the verifier walks one canonical-bytes algorithm regardless of leaf_type.
